completed_tasks.task: replaced http.server.ThreadingHTTPServer with plain http.server.HTTPServer in GatewayRestServer -- requests now handled one at a time, in full, before the next is accepted, eliminating shared-mutable-GatewaySession races

added tests/contract/test_gateway_rest_concurrency.py: no overlap in GatewaySession.handle under concurrent client threads (instrumented probe), accepted concurrent requests map 1:1 to ToolFacade calls, log sequence numbers unique/contiguous, ledger commits never race (8 concurrent writes -> exactly 8 distinct effects), report submission cannot race a consequential write, finalization cannot observe an in-flight operation, two independent runs converge on the same final state
completed_tasks.evidence: 7 tests, stable across 3 repeated local runs; commit 5b04f3b

completed_tasks.task: hardened GatewayRestServer lifecycle: created->running->stopped state tracking; stop() before start() no longer hangs (only closes the socket, never calls shutdown()); start() while running is idempotent no-op; start() after stop() raises new ServerLifecycleError; stop() idempotent, server_close() exactly once

added tests/unit/test_gateway_rest_lifecycle.py: every risky call bounded by a daemon-thread timeout helper so a regression fails loudly instead of hanging the suite -- covers stop-before-start, start-idempotent, start-after-stop error, stop-idempotent, normal and exception-path context-manager cleanup, server_close called exactly once
updated docs (architecture.md 'Request serialization' + 'Server lifecycle' sections, rest-mapping.md intro), DECISION_LOG.md D-021, CHANGELOG.md

completed_tasks.task: added _HandshakingHTTPServer (HTTPServer subclass overriding service_actions(), the documented per-loop-iteration extension point called after select() returns) to signal a threading.Event the first time serve_forever()'s loop genuinely runs -- closes the startup race where start() previously returned before serve_forever() had actually begun

start()/stop() now share one threading.Lock for their entire call (including bounded waits); start() blocks on running_confirmed (bounded by _STARTUP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=5.0, raising ServerLifecycleError and tearing the server down on timeout) before returning; stop() only calls shutdown() when _state=='running', which is now only true after the handshake confirmed

added 6 new lifecycle tests: 30x rapid start-then-stop; competing start()/stop() threads (both valid lock-ordering outcomes accepted); stop() arriving during a deliberately slowed startup handshake; simulated startup timeout via monkeypatched _STARTUP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (stays fast, no real 5s wait); no leaked server thread or listening socket after stop()
completed_tasks.evidence: tests/unit/test_gateway_rest_lifecycle.py, now 13 tests total, stable across 5 repeated local runs; commit c351400

completed_tasks.task: corrected the concurrency contract wording across code and docs: requests never overlap and are processed sequentially; processing order is whatever HTTPServer actually accepted connections in, not a gateway-imposed or reproducible order; a reproducible ordered candidate trace requires the candidate to send one request at a time and wait for each response; no queue ordering, batching, sorting, or parallel execution introduced
completed_tasks.evidence: src/cavbench/gateway/rest.py module docstring 'The concurrency contract, precisely'; docs/program/gateway/architecture.md 'Request serialization'; docs/program/gateway/rest-mapping.md; DECISION_LOG.md D-021; commits c351400 (rest.py), 3848781 (test wording), 2df48a9 (docs)

completed_tasks.task: replaced serve_forever()/shutdown() with _ManagedHTTPServer.run(), a loop over the public handle_request() primitive cancelled via an always-safe-to-signal threading.Event (request_cancellation()) instead of serve_forever()'s private shutdown handshake; running_confirmed is set as the first statement inside run(), authoritative proof the loop started

added one internal GatewayRestServer._cleanup(): signal cancellation, join the thread with a bounded timeout, close the socket exactly once (guarded by a flag independent of _state); returns whether the thread was actually confirmed dead. start()'s timeout path and stop() both call it -- exactly one place that can leak a thread or socket

start()'s timeout path now raises a distinct ServerLifecycleError naming both the startup failure and 'could not be terminated' if cleanup's own bounded join cannot confirm the thread died, rather than silently claiming a teardown that did not happen
completed_tasks.evidence: smoke-tested directly: Unterminable test double correctly produces the 'could not be terminated' error while accurately reporting thread_alive=True at that moment

completed_tasks.task: added private _server_class constructor parameter to GatewayRestServer so tests install a server double before any socket is bound, replacing the prior post-construction server._httpd = ... swap that leaked the originally-constructed default server's socket

rewrote tests/unit/test_gateway_rest_lifecycle.py: fixed the leaking test-injection pattern in the two existing tests that used it, and added tests for startup-timeout leaves-no-live-thread, stop-after-startup-failure-harmless-and-repeatable (no second server_close), bounded cleanup timing, honest 'could not be terminated' reporting, 10x repeated startup failures compared against threading.enumerate() before/after, and injected-test-server-does-not-leak-the-default. stop()-during-startup race now synchronized via a threading.Event, not a bare sleep
completed_tasks.evidence: 18 tests total (was 13), all pass; commit ffad415; stable across 5 repeated local runs
